Interface ISymbolicAssociation

All Superinterfaces:
ISymbolicElement
All Known Implementing Classes:
SymbolicAssociation, SymbolicLayoutReader.KeYlessAssociation
public interface ISymbolicAssociation extends ISymbolicElement

Represents an association of an ISymbolicState or ISymbolicObject which is a reference to an ISymbolicObject.

The default implementation is SymbolicAssociation.

See Also:
  • SymbolicAssociation

  • Method Details

    • getName

      String getName()
      Returns a human readable name.
      Returns:
      A human readable name.

    • isArrayIndex

      boolean isArrayIndex()
      Checks if an array index or a program variable is represented.
      Returns:
      true array index, false program variable.

    • getArrayIndex

      JTerm getArrayIndex()
      Returns the represented array index or null if a program variable is represented..
      Returns:
      The represented array index or null if a program variable is represented..

    • getArrayIndexString

      String getArrayIndexString()
      Returns the human readable array index or null if a program variable is represented..
      Returns:
      The human readable array index or null if a program variable is represented..

    • getProgramVariable

      IProgramVariable getProgramVariable()
      Returns the represented IProgramVariable.
      Returns:
      The represented IProgramVariable.

    • getProgramVariableString

      String getProgramVariableString()
      Returns the represented IProgramVariable as human readable String.
      Returns:
      The represented IProgramVariable as human readable String.

    • getTarget

      ISymbolicObject getTarget()
      Returns the target ISymbolicObject.
      Returns:
      The target ISymbolicObject.

    • getCondition

      JTerm getCondition()

      Returns the optional condition under which this association is valid.

      The condition should be null by default. Only in rare cases, e.g. path condition is not strong enough to describe the path completely, is a condition is provided.

      Returns:
      The optional condition under which this association is valid.

    • getConditionString

      String getConditionString()

      Returns the optional condition under which this association is valid as human readable String.

      The condition should be null by default. Only in rare cases, e.g. path condition is not strong enough to describe the path completely, is a condition is provided.

      Returns:
      The optional condition under which this association is valid as human readable String.
